Are genes of interest expression distributions different in long/short OS in TSI-stroma regions?

Are genes of interest expression distributions different in long/short OS in TSI-stroma regions?

Are genes of interest expression distributions different in long/short PFS in TSI-stroma regions?

What are biological functions of differentially expressed genes in long-short survivors in TSI-stroma regions?

Cell Cycle Regulation: 
Mitotic Cell Cycle: Highly enriched (Fold Enrichment = 7.96), significant (p-value = 1.03E-10). 
Cell Cycle Process: Enriched (Fold Enrichment = 5.53), highly significant (p-value = 6.28E-09). 
Regulation of Cell Cycle: Enriched (Fold Enrichment = 5.98), significant (p-value = 2.08E-08). 
Chromosome and DNA Processes: 
Chromosome Segregation: Enriched (Fold Enrichment = 7.81), significant (p-value = 2.16E-06). 
DNA Replication: Significant enrichment (Fold Enrichment = 8.78), (p-value = 6.28E-05). 
Mitotic Regulation: 
Regulation of Mitotic Cell Cycle Phase Transition: Highly enriched (Fold Enrichment = 8.94), significant (p-value = 1.61E-07). 
Negative Regulation of Mitotic Cell Cycle: Significant enrichment (Fold Enrichment = 9.94), (p-value = 6.60E-06).

Are genes of interest differently expressed in TSI-stroma fibroblasts in long/short PFS/OS group?

Prognostic significance of genes of interest in TSI-stroma fibroblasts: ML-based approach for OS prediction

Implemented scikit-sklearn RandomForestClassifier() for classifying segments as “Low OS” or “High OS” group based on genes of interest expression.
Accuracy around 0.8 depending on the random seed.

Implemented scikit-sklearn RandomForestClassifier() for classifying segments as “Low PFS” or “High PFS” group based on genes of interest expression.
Accuracy around 0.8 depending on the random seed

Prognostic significance of genes of interest in TSI-stroma fibroblasts: ML-based approach for OS prediction

Prognostic significance of genes of interest in TSI-stroma fibroblasts: ML-based approach for PFS prediction

Prognostic significance of genes of interest in TSI-stroma regions: Cox models hazard ratio estimation based on the PFS

Conclusions

Proportions of several cell types are significantly different between TSI-stroma and stroma-stroma regions.
there is no statistically significant differences in DEG analysis between tsi-stroma and stroma-stroma fibroblasts.
mitotic pathways are enriched in the DEG lists when comparing TSI-stroma fibroblasts of long- and short-term survivors.
ML model integration with fibroblast-originating gene of interest expression can offer prognostic value.

Thank you for your attention!
